source: BEP Research — public pricing pages, earnings calls (Q4 FY2025/Q1 FY2026), SEC filings, press releases
methodology: Token Cost Waterfall maps the margin stack from GPU silicon to end-customer. The platform 'token margin' is (customer price per interaction − blended model cost per interaction) / customer price. modelCost is now DERIVED LIVE from data/token-pricing/current.json by weighting each platform's modelMix against today's market output prices; proprietary in-house models use a stated marginal-cost estimate. Confidence levels: HIGH = public pricing page, MEDIUM = earnings call disclosure + estimate, LOW = analyst inference. Token-margin EXCLUDES hosting, R&D, sales, and support — it isolates the input-cost variable that compounds with LLMflation.
tokenMarginCaveat: Platform 'token margin' is gross margin on the token-cost line only. It is NOT company-level gross margin. Hosting, fine-tuning, customer support, sales commissions, and agent R&D sit on top of token cost and reduce true gross margin. Read as: 'of every dollar of platform revenue, X cents goes to model providers.'

platforms.name: Agentforce
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-model routing. xGen for dev tasks, GPT-5/GPT-4o default, Claude via Bedrock. BYOLLM supported.

customerPricing.altPricing: $0.10/action via Flex Credits (May 2025). Also per-user at $125-650/user/month.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: ~10K tokens/conversation (system prompt + RAG context + reasoning + response). xGen routing reduces cost on simpler queries.

margins.notes: 3.2 trillion tokens processed (Q3 FY26). $1.8B ARR Agentforce + Data Cloud, 114% YoY. 9,500+ paid deals.

platforms.name: AI Agents
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-LLM failover for latency. Routes simple intents to cheaper models.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 2-5K tokens per resolution. Only charged on full resolution (no human handoff).

margins.notes: Private (Hellman & Friedman, $10.2B take-private 2022). Dynamic pricing plan released 2025 allows remixing seats vs resolutions.

platforms.dataGap: Private company — no revenue disclosure post-2022 privatization.

platforms.name: Fin AI Agent
platforms.modelProviderNote: Switched from OpenAI to Anthropic Claude for Fin 2 (Oct 2024). Fin Apex 1.0 (proprietary post-trained) beats GPT-5.4 and Claude Sonnet 4.6 on CS benchmarks (73.1% resolution rate). Apex included at no extra cost.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Apex 1.0 handles ~50% of resolutions at near-zero marginal cost. Claude tier-2 handles complex cases.

margins.notes: Proprietary Apex 1.0 is the margin-expansion play. Anthropic itself is a Fin customer. Private.

platforms.dataGap: Private company — no revenue or margin disclosure.

platforms.name: Now Assist
platforms.modelProviderNote: Claude as default for Build Agent (Jan 2026 Anthropic partnership). Open ecosystem — customers can choose LLMs.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 10 AI interactions/day × 6K tokens. Heavy routing to cheaper models for classification/triage.

margins.notes: Now Assist ACV >$600M (Q4 FY2025), tracking $1B by year-end 2026. 31% non-GAAP op margin.

platforms.dataGap: Per-user pricing not publicly listed. ACV disclosed but per-seat breakdown estimated.

platforms.name: M365 Copilot
platforms.modelProviderNote: Microsoft owns 49% of OpenAI. Exclusive cloud provider. Captive supply at sub-API cost.

customerPricing.altPricing: Business/SMB: $21/user/month. Copilot Pro: $20/month. M365 E3 rising to $39/user/month July 2026.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 12 interactions/day × 4K tokens. 50% internal discount via equity stake.

margins.notes: Microsoft effectively pays itself for inference via OpenAI. M365 base price increase ($36→$39, July 2026) absorbs AI cost.

platforms.name: GitHub Copilot
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-model. Power users hit premium-request caps to limit token exposure.

customerPricing.altPricing: Pro+ at $39/user/month. Enterprise at $39+. Free tier with 2K completions/month.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Heavy users: 25 completions/day × 6K tokens. Light users (~8/day) are profitable. Premium model caps mitigate worst-case loss.

margins.notes: GitHub disclosed Copilot 'approaching profitability' in aggregate (late 2025). Request caps introduced 2026 to control cost.

platforms.dataGap: Actual per-user token consumption varies 10x between light and heavy users.

platforms.name: Joule AI
platforms.modelProviderNote: AI Unit pricing intentionally opaque — obscures token economics.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: AI Unit → token mapping not disclosed. Opaque pricing unit prevents reliable margin computation.

margins.notes: Cloud gross margin ~73%. SAP Cloud revenue EUR 4.7B/quarter, 27% YoY.

platforms.dataGap: AI Unit to token conversion unknown. Cannot compute per-token margin.

platforms.name: Workspace + Gemini
platforms.modelProviderNote: Vertically integrated TPU → Gemini → Workspace. Lowest cost producer.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 8 interactions/day × 3K tokens. 60% TPU-internal discount.

margins.notes: Folding AI into base subscription is penetration strategy — monetizes through retention.

platforms.name: Fusion Applications AI
platforms.modelProviderNote: Cohere primary via strategic partnership. Oracle proprietary models for specific domains.

customerPricing.altPricing: Bundled into Fusion Cloud subscriptions; AI premium ~20% uplift.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 6 interactions/day × 5K tokens. Domain models handle structured ERP queries.

margins.notes: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure revenue $7.1B (Q3 FY26). OCI margin ~38%.

platforms.dataGap: AI premium not separately disclosed; per-user pricing inferred from partner contracts.

platforms.name: Firefly + GenStudio
platforms.modelProviderNote: Adobe Firefly is image diffusion; LLM portion uses Anthropic. Credits monthly grant in CC.

customerPricing.altPricing: 1000 credits/month included in Creative Cloud All Apps ($60/month).

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Image generation dominates compute. Text portion 2K tokens of prompt+caption.

margins.notes: Firefly trained on licensed Adobe Stock corpus — defensible IP. Adobe DM segment 88% gross margin.

platforms.dataGap: Image generation isn't strictly token-priced. Credits map to GPU-seconds, not tokens.

platforms.name: Breeze
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-LLM under Breeze Intelligence umbrella. Free Breeze tier in all paid plans; premium agents charged separately.

customerPricing.altPricing: Breeze Intelligence credits $30/month. Free Breeze Copilot in all paid plans.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 8 interactions/day × 4.5K tokens for typical CRM workflow.

margins.notes: HubSpot Q1 FY26 revenue $703M, 81% gross margin. Breeze cited as growth driver.

platforms.name: Rovo
platforms.modelProviderNote: Free Rovo Chat in all paid Cloud plans (Feb 2026). Premium agents charged separately.

customerPricing.altPricing: Rovo Chat included free with Cloud plans. Agents add-on at $20/user/month.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Margin computed against the $20 paid Agents tier. Free Chat is platform investment.

margins.notes: Free Rovo Chat is platform-lock play. Atlassian Q3 FY26 Cloud revenue $1.45B, 71% YoY. Cloud gross margin ~85%.

platforms.dataGap: Free Rovo Chat distribution makes per-user margin ambiguous. Premium Agents not yet at scale.

platforms.name: Notion AI
platforms.modelProviderNote: Primary on Anthropic Claude. Built directly on frontier APIs, no proprietary layer.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 6 interactions/day × 4K tokens. Notion uses long context (full page content) which inflates input tokens.

margins.notes: Private (last raised at $10B Apr 2024). No proprietary model — fully exposed to model-provider pricing.

platforms.dataGap: Private company. Token margin highly sensitive to Anthropic pricing.

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise
platforms.modelProviderNote: First-party — no margin leakage to external model provider. Token cost = internal GPU cost only.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 20 interactions/day × 8K tokens (enterprise power users). 70% internal discount (GPU cost vs API list price).

margins.notes: OpenAI is first-party — pure GPU economics, no model-provider markup.

platforms.dataGap: Enterprise pricing custom-negotiated; $60/user/month is midpoint estimate.

platforms.name: Cortex AI
platforms.modelProvider: Anthropic + Meta + Snowflake Arctic + customer choice
platforms.modelProviderNote: Cortex offers Claude, Llama, Arctic, Mistral. Customer pays Snowflake credits which include compute + model markup.

customerPricing.altPricing: Per-token: $0.0006-$0.024/1K input tokens depending on model.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Snowflake marks up model API rate ~2-3x via credit system. Compute included.

margins.notes: Snowflake Q1 FY26 product revenue $1.04B, 32% YoY. Cortex AI fastest-growing component.

platforms.name: Mosaic AI
platforms.modelProvider: Anthropic + Meta + Mosaic DBRX + customer choice
platforms.modelProviderNote: Foundation Model APIs include DBRX, Llama, Claude, Mixtral. Mosaic ML acquired 2023 ($1.3B).

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Mosaic markup ~2x model API rate. DBRX self-hosted at marginal cost.

margins.notes: Databricks private ($62B valuation). Reported $3B+ ARR, ~35% YoY.

platforms.dataGap: Private. DBU-to-token conversion varies by workload type.

platforms.name: AIP
platforms.modelProviderNote: AIP is orchestration layer over multiple LLMs. Multi-million-dollar enterprise contracts, not per-token.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Token consumption not the binding cost — Palantir margin dominated by Forward Deployed Engineers.

margins.notes: Palantir Q1 FY26 commercial revenue $307M (+71%). US commercial AIP driving growth.

platforms.dataGap: AIP value proposition is not token-priced — engagement model dominates. Token margin N/A.

platforms.name: Cursor
platforms.modelProviderNote: Cursor Pro at $20/user/month. Heavy Claude routing for code edits.

customerPricing.altPricing: Free tier with 2K completions/month. Business at $40/user/month.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Power-user heavy. 30 completions/day × 7K tokens. Significant agentic loops.

margins.notes: Anysphere private ($9.9B valuation, May 2026). $300M+ ARR reported. Growth fast, margin tight.

platforms.dataGap: Private. Token consumption per power user could be 2-3x stated assumption.

platforms.name: Grok for Business
platforms.modelProviderNote: First-party. xAI operates own GPU clusters (Colossus).

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 15 interactions/day × 6K tokens. First-party GPU cost only.

margins.notes: xAI valuation ~$200B (May 2026). Capex-heavy: Colossus 2 supercomputer cited as $5B+ buildout.

platforms.dataGap: Private. Enterprise pricing not formally published.

platforms.name: Box AI
platforms.modelProvider: Anthropic + OpenAI + AWS Bedrock + Box Hubs
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-LLM agentic platform. Box AI Studio launched 2024.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: 4 interactions/day × 5K tokens (document analysis is context-heavy).

margins.notes: Box Q1 FY26 revenue $277M, 75% gross margin. AI cited as 20% of new ARR.

platforms.dataGap: AI portion of Enterprise Plus pricing inferred from tier delta.

historicalCostBenchmark.description: Blended frontier output $/M output by quarter, used to back-test margin compounding. Index derived from LLMflation history.

waterfall_summary.gpu_to_token: GPU TCO $/hr ÷ tokens/sec ÷ 3600 × 1M = production cost per million tokens. H100 TCO ~$3.00/hr ÷ 95 tok/s/GPU = ~$8.77/M (Llama 70B GPU-only baseline).
waterfall_summary.token_to_api: Frontier labs sell at 1.5-3x production cost. Anthropic Sonnet 4.6 at $15/M output. Commodity floor ($0.18/M for gpt-oss-120b) is sold at a loss to capture marketshare.
waterfall_summary.api_to_platform: Enterprise platforms charge 20-100x model API cost per interaction. Per-resolution platforms (Zendesk, Intercom) and per-conversation platforms (Salesforce) compound margin fastest because their unit economics are decoupled from token deflation.
compression_points: Per-resolution platforms (Zendesk, Intercom, Salesforce) are insulated from token deflation — they sell outcomes, not tokens. Margin EXPANDS as input cost falls.
Per-user platforms (Microsoft, ServiceNow, HubSpot, Notion) face power-user cost risk. Token consumption scales with user behavior, not contract value.
Proprietary model development (Intercom Apex, Salesforce xGen, Adobe Firefly, Snowflake Arctic, Databricks DBRX) is the durable margin-expansion play.
Consumption-credit platforms (Snowflake, Databricks) sit in between — capture compute markup but expose customers to token volatility.
Free-AI platforms (Atlassian Rovo Chat, Google Workspace) sacrifice direct AI margin to defend platform lock-in.
arbitrage_opportunities: Salesforce charges $2/conversation against ~$0.06 token cost — 33x markup. Any focused vertical agent (legal, healthcare) can undercut massively.
GitHub Copilot at $19/user/month is loss-making for power users. Cap introduction signals Microsoft is finding the profitability boundary.
Cursor at $20/user/month with 30 completions/day is likely token-negative. Anysphere's $9.9B valuation requires margin expansion via proprietary models or pricing increases.
ServiceNow moving to consumption-based pricing — signals confidence that per-token margins are positive.
Google folding Gemini into base Workspace at $4/user implied AI revenue — accepting lower per-AI margin to block competitors.
Notion AI fully exposed to Anthropic pricing (no proprietary model). Margin compresses if Claude rates rise.
Snowflake and Databricks both running ~2-3x model API markup via credit system — durable middleman position as long as data gravity holds.
waterfall_summary.key_finding: Margin concentrates at the distribution layer, not the production layer. Per-resolution platforms (Zendesk, Intercom, Salesforce) capture the highest token-margins because they sell outcomes, not tokens. Per-user platforms face power-user cost risk. The investable insight: the enterprise platform layer earns a 20-100x markup on model-provider cost on the token line — but that 'margin' is gross-of-everything-else (hosting, R&D, sales). The structural advantage is durability: per-resolution pricing decouples revenue from input cost, so as LLMflation continues, margins compound. Proprietary models are the second moat.
